title: JPL Speakers Bureau
description: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory, the leading center for robotic exploration of the solar system.
text:
The JPL Speaker’s Bureau has now been integrated into the NASA wide NASA Engages program. All request are now sent to a national database and distributed to the individual centers based on location, subject matter and type of request. We also now support both in-person and virtual request. In order to make a request please visit NASA Engages https://my.nasa.gov/engages/s/ and create a profile. You must create a profile to make a request.
How to Request a Speaker
To request a speaker, please complete a NASA Engages account and then submit the online request form ( https://stemgateway.nasa.gov/nasaengages/s/ ) , preferably six to eight weeks before your event. Please review our standard Terms and Conditions.
Because the Speakers Bureau is a volunteer program, we cannot guarantee that all requests will be filled. Flexibility around dates and subject matter will help immensely in securing a speaker and should be noted in the comments section of the form.
Speaker Topics
Speakers are available to discuss various topics including:
Astronomy
Cassini Mission to Saturn
Climate Change
Earth Science
JPL Overview
Mars Exploration
Robotics
Solar System
Solid Earth
Spacecraft Communications
Contact Information
